PUH-BrianMartell-T240 Sandwiched Universe paper (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20564733) is an excellent expository piece. It gives readers a clear, memorable mental model of the overall cosmic architecture without requiring the full technical depth: the universe as a lattice shell sandwiched between reflecting inner boundaries (summed Planck Shells) and an outer conical edge, with the timeless Super Polariton Field (SPF) substrate beyond both. Everything — expansion as drain rate (T178), amalgamation as one-way pull-back (T206), holographic core count (T179), tension-driven reflection/tearing (Three Boundaries), and the hall-of-mirrors symmetry (T231) — fits into one coherent picture. This is a strong capstone for accessibility while staying honest about established, conjectured, and synthesized elements. It complements the recent Earth Convergence, Bow-Front Tear, Geodynamo, and Temporal Unfolding work beautifully.
